note: Advisory sandbox detector. Emits CHECK_OK/CHECK_REJECTED verdicts, never a bare governance PASS. Read-only over the two ground-truth data files; exits NONZERO on any stale-law hit (not an always-exit-0 stub).
title: Stale-Law Sweep over ACTIVE agent read surfaces (static, read-only)

subject.note: sweeps the 5 ACTIVE-applicable stale-law regexes across 31 in-repo ACTIVE must_read surfaces; both ground-truth files read-only, sha unchanged

result.interpretation: No ACTIVE agent read surface carries a stale-law string. The clean result is trustworthy because the hardcoded positive control (synthetic known-bad surface) is detected every run and a minimal mutation of a real surface (AGENTS.md + 'Vercel') is flagged. The pattern-source file is excluded as the detector's own rule table (it self-matches 6x by construction: Vercel/vercel_deploy/NOOS-for-Vercel in its regex/replacement/message metadata) — this is scoping, not relaxation, and is printed transparently.

what_it_proves: REAL ACTIVE read surfaces (31 in-repo must_read files) -> CHECK_OK, zero stale-law hits; positive control detected so the clean result is not a broken-regex false-clean

POSITIVE CONTROL: a hardcoded synthetic surface known to contain stale-law strings trips 4 ACTIVE patterns every run; if it stopped matching the tool returns CHECK_REJECTED (a zero-hit can never be reported clean when the regexes are dead)

NEGATIVE FIXTURE by MINIMAL MUTATION of a real on-disk surface (AGENTS.md + one injected 'Vercel' token) -> FLAGGED, cites surface + pattern_id, exits nonzero (RED); the unmutated original is clean (GREEN)
workflow-glob pattern (vercel_workflow_file) is gated to *vercel*.yml workflow surfaces only — it does not broad-sweep the substring 'vercel' across arbitrary data files
running the sweep leaves both ground-truth files byte-identical (sha unchanged); tool is strictly read-only
verdict vocab CHECK_OK/CHECK_REJECTED, never a bare governance PASS; exits NONZERO on any hit (not always-exit-0)
chess_patches_applied: runs against the REAL patterns + real read-surface registry (no strawman); 31 genuine surfaces swept
positive control + minimal-mutation-of-real-artifact provide RED-before-GREEN; no pattern weakening
pattern-source file excluded as the detector's own rule table (self-match by construction), stated transparently in output and receipt — scoping, not relaxation to hide a real hit
origin=sandbox-advisory / authority=none; reports hits as a finding, never its own PASS
locks_respected: Lock 5 append-only (both ground-truth files unmodified, sha unchanged)
no live call (no network/Supabase/deploy; pure static grep)
read-only detector; writes only inside catalog/builds/MO-7/
worktree not pushed; no git write commands

results.real_surfaces: CHECK_OK exit 0 (0 hits, 31 surfaces, positive-control OK)
results.red_canary: FIRED (wrong 'surfaces are clean' assertion fails on mutated-real + positive-control hits)

exit_gate_B3: MO-7 done (stale-law sweep: real ACTIVE surfaces clean CHECK_OK; RED-before-GREEN via hardcoded positive control + minimal-mutation-of-real-surface negative fixture; workflow-glob pattern gated; pattern-source self-match excluded transparently).
next_action: None required from this finding — all ACTIVE read surfaces are stale-law clean. Keep MO-7 in the motor set to re-sweep on every surface/law change so a future retired-law string on an ACTIVE surface trips CHECK_REJECTED immediately.
